specific_mechanism: FP-Temper: Fokker-Planck-Calibrated Tempered SGD. Estimate effective diffusion Σ_batch(θ) from mini-batch gradient variance. Choose target temperature T. Inject corrective Gaussian noise ξ ~ N(0, max(0, T I - Σ_batch) ) so total diffusion = T I. Stationary distribution under FP becomes exp(-L(θ)/T). Anneal T from T_max → T_min as schedule.
llm_application: FP-Temper: at each SGD step measure per-coordinate batch-gradient variance σ²_i(θ); inject corrective Gaussian noise ξ_i ~ N(0, max(0, T - σ²_i)) so total per-coord diffusion equals T uniformly. As T anneals over training, the trajectory samples loss valleys at temperature T. Use this to fine-tune toward flat minima by holding T moderate before final T → 0 collapse.
novelty_claim: SGLD (Welling-Teh 2011) and SGD-as-Bayesian-Inference (Mandt-Hoffman-Blei 2017) cover the foundational FP-SGD link. FP-Temper's per-coord corrective noise targeting uniform diffusion is a specific instantiation. The broader FP-LLM area is well-covered.
motivation_justification: Fokker-Planck IS the PDE of SGD as SDE. The candidate transfers the closed-form Gibbs stationary identity.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: Per-coord mini-batch noise variance estimation
M_3: Target uniform diffusion T I
content_words: fokker planck equation stationary distribution gibbs
Gibbs sample target loss valley exploration
